info.name: Ali Real Estate Chatbot

request.description: Liveness probe. Returns server status, active WebSocket connection count, TTS availability, and timestamp.
Create a new conversation session. The returned session_id is automatically saved as a collection variable for use in subsequent requests.
Retrieve the current state of a session: stage, selected category, subtype, price, and turn count.
Delete a session immediately. Returns 404 if the session does not exist or has expired.

formdata.description: Audio file to transcribe (WebM, WAV, MP3, OGG, etc.)

request.description: Upload an audio file for speech-to-text transcription using faster-whisper. Returns {"text": "<transcribed string>"}.

WebSocket endpoint for real-time streaming chat.

Connect via: ws://localhost:8000/ws/chat

Send JSON:
{
  "session_id": "<uuid>",
  "message": "I want to buy a house",
  "voice": true
}

Receive JSON frames:
- {"type": "token", "data": "<token>"}         — streamed LLM tokens
- {"type": "audio_chunk", "data": "<base64>"}   — TTS audio (when voice=true)
- {"type": "done", "data": ""}                  — end of turn
- {"type": "state", "data": {session_info}}      — updated session state
- {"type": "session_created", "data": "<id>"}    — auto-created session
- {"type": "error", "data": "<message>"}         — error

Note: Postman's HTTP client cannot test WebSockets directly. Use the Postman WebSocket Request tab or a tool like wscat:
  wscat -c ws://localhost:8000/ws/chat
